name: Unreal AI GAS
description: An AI Game Developer (Unreal-MCP) extension contributing MCP tools an AI agent can call against the Unreal Editor.

tools.name: gas-list-abilities
tools.description: Lists every Gameplay Ability class (UGameplayAbility subclass — native C++ and Blueprint) known to the project via the Asset Registry, without loading any of them. Optionally filter by a class-path prefix.

tools.name: gas-list-effects
tools.description: Lists every Gameplay Effect class (UGameplayEffect subclass — native C++ and Blueprint) known to the project via the Asset Registry, without loading any of them. Optionally filter by a class-path prefix.

tools.name: gas-list-attribute-sets
tools.description: Lists every Attribute Set class (UAttributeSet subclass — native C++ and Blueprint) known to the project via the Asset Registry, without loading any of them. Optionally filter by a class-path prefix.

tools.name: gas-inspect-ability
tools.description: Inspects a single Gameplay Ability class (read-only) by loading its class default object and reporting its configuration: ability/cancel/block gameplay tags, the cost and cooldown Gameplay Effect classes, and the instancing policy.

tools.name: gas-grant-ability
tools.description: Grants a Gameplay Ability to a named actor's AbilitySystemComponent in the active editor world (the actor must already own an AbilitySystemComponent).

_comment: Install-catalog / compatibility manifest, shaped as ONE unreal-mcp-cli ExtensionDescriptor (cli/src/utils/extensions-catalog.ts in Unreal-MCP) so it is directly liftable into the shared extensions.catalog.json { schemaVersion, extensions[] }. 'version' mirrors the .uplugin VersionName (kept in sync by commands/bump-version.ps1). 'minCoreVersion' is the lowest Unreal-MCP core version this extension supports (refreshed by commands/update-core.ps1). 'enginePlugins' are the gating engine plugins (e.g. ['Niagara']) enabled in the .uproject alongside this extension — populated by commands/init -FeaturePlugin. 'tools' lists the contributed tool ids/descriptions. The install-extension resolver unions enginePlugins with the materialized .uplugin Plugins[].
